turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Alito has the opinion of the Court this morning, case 23-971, Waetzig v. Halliburton Energy Services.

turns.name: JUSTICE ALITO
turns.text: Some of you have probably come here this morning because you want to learn more about Rule 60(b) of the Rules of Civil Procedure and if you are in that group, you are in luck. If you are not in that group, this will be brief. Rule 60(b) permits a court to relieve a party from a "final judgment order or proceeding." The question in this case is whether the rule permits a court to reopen a case that was voluntarily dismissed without prejudice under Rule 41(a). We hold that such a dismissal without prejudice counts as a final proceeding and thus qualifies for Rule 60(b) relief. A voluntary dismissal without prejudice is final because it terminates the case in which it is filed and it is a proceeding because that term, as used in the federal rules, generally refers to any step taken in an action. So, for this reason, the judgment of the Tenth Circuit is reversed, and the case is remanded for further proceedings consistent with this opinion. The opinion of the Court -- the decision of the Court, is unanimous.
